zh.lifeSummary: 當John Payne於1595年9月29日出生在英國賀福爾亨廷頓時，他的父親Thomas Payne Sr51歲，母親Maria 12GGM Mary Watterton Payne50歲。他於1620年8月3日與Anna Underhill在英國亨廷格多史爾海爾 韋斯頓結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1635年9月20日在英國英格蘭賀福爾亨廷頓享年39歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当John Payne于1595年9月29日出生在英国賀福爾亨廷頓时，他的父亲Thomas Payne Sr51岁，母亲Maria 12GGM Mary Watterton Payne50岁。他于1620年8月3日与Anna Underhill在英国亨廷格多史爾海爾 韋斯頓结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1635年9月20日在英国英格兰賀福爾亨廷頓享年39岁，埋葬在英国英格兰。

nameMeanings.name: Payne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English and Old French personal name Pai(e)n, Pagen (from Latin Paganus), a fairly common personal name among Normans. It derived from a word that originally meant ‘villager, rustic’, later ‘heathen’, but it had doubtless lost these connotations in its use as a late medieval personal name. This name has also been established in Ireland since the 14th century.

History: Thomas Payne, who was a freeman of Plymouth Colony in 1639, was the founder of a large American family, which included Robert Treat Paine (1731–1814), one of the signers of the Declaration of Independence. See also   Paine  .

variants.name: Payn
meanings: English (Kent): variant of   Payne  .

variants.name: Payen
meanings: French: from the Old French personal name Pagen, Paien, from Latin Paganus, from paganus ‘country-dweller’, a derivative of pagus ‘of or relating to the countryside’, possibly given to a child not yet baptized (because of its later sense of ‘pagan, heathen’). Alternatively, a nickname for a country-born person. This surname is also found in the West Indies (mainly Haiti) and Mauritius.
English: variant of   Payne  .

variants.name: Pagan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Luis, Juan, Angel, Carlos, Rafael, Ramon, Pedro, Francisco, Jesus, Miguel, Julio.

Spanish and Catalan: Castilianized form (Pagán) of Catalan Pagà, from the Late Latin personal name Paganus, which as a derivative of Latin pagus ‘outlying village’ originally meant ‘villager’ (see Payne ). Alternatively, in some cases, a topographic name from Catalan pagà ‘heath’ (from the same Latin word pagus).
English: from the Middle English and Old French personal name Pai(e)n, Pagen, from Late Latin Paganus (see Payne , compare 1 above).

variants.name: Paynes
meanings: English: variant of Payne , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Paine
meanings: English: variant of   Payne  .

History: The author of the republican treatise The Rights of Man, Thomas Paine (1737–1809), left England for North America in the mid 1770s, where he became involved in the movement that led to independence. His pamphlet of 1776, Common Sense, influenced the Declaration of Independence and furnished some of the arguments justifying it.

variants.name: Pank
meanings: English (Norfolk): perhaps from an unrecorded Middle English personal name Pank(e). It could be a shortened form of Pancok, which is probably a pet form of Pane (see Payne ).
Sorbian: nickname from a diminutive of pan ‘mister, lord, landlord’ (see Pan ). Compare Panke .

variants.name: Pane
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Antonio, Salvatore, Aldo, Carmela, Carmine, Ciro, Concetta, Enzo, Giuseppe, Mario, Armando, Claudio, Macario, Remigio.

Italian: from pane ‘bread’, used as a metonymic occupational name for a baker or as a nickname meaning ‘good thing’, and also as a personal name. This surname is also found in southern France and Corsica.
English: variant of   Payne  .

variants.name: Panes
meanings: English (Somerset): variant of Payne , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Pannell
meanings: English (southeastern):
possibly from Middle English panel, panel(l)e ‘cloth, saddle-cloth, saddle’ or ‘pane (of glass)’, hence a metonymic occupational name for a maker of such items.
from the Middle English personal name Paynel (Old French Payenel, Latin Paganellus), a pet form of Payne (from Latin Paganus).

variants.name: Penson
meanings: English:
patronymic from the Middle English female personal name Penn, a pet form of Pernel, or perhaps from the Middle English personal name Payne + -son. See Penn , Parnell .

habitational name from Penson in North Huish (Devon), which derives from the Anglo-Norman French personal name Payn(e) (see Payne ) + Middle English toun ‘village, town, settlement’ (Old English tūn ‘farmstead, estate’).

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When John Payne was born on 29 September 1595, in Huntington, Herefordshire, England, his father, Thomas Payne Sr, was 51 and his mother, Maria 12GGM Mary Watterton Payne, was 50. He married Anna Underhill on 3 August 1620, in Hail Weston, Huntingdonshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. He died on 20 September 1635, in Huntington, Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 39, and was buried in England, United Kingdom.

Il a épousé Anna Underhill le 3 août 1620, à Hail Weston, Huntingdonshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 20 septembre 1635, à Huntington, Herefordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 39 ans, et a été enterré à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ko.lifeSummary: John Payne(이)가 1595년 9월 29일 영국 허퍼드실르 헌틴그턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas Payne Sr(은)는 51세였고, 그의 어머니 Maria 12GGM Mary Watterton Payne(은)는 50세였다. 그는 1620년 8월 3일 영국 헌틴그도실르 하일 웨스턴에서 Anna Underhill(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1635년 9월 20일 영국 잉글랜드 허퍼드실르 헌틴그턴에서 39세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드에 묻혔다.

Ele casou-se com Anna Underhill em 3 de agosto de 1620, em Hail Weston, Huntingdonshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. ru.St Giles Cripplegate, London, England: ст джилесь витоут крипплегэит, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда John Payne родился 29 сентября 1595 г. в местности хантингтон, хирефордшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Thomas Payne Sr было 51 год, а матери, которую звали Maria 12GGM Mary Watterton Payne, 50. Он женился на женщине по имени Anna Underhill 3 августа 1620 г. в местности хеил вестон, хантингдоншир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Он умер 20 сентября 1635 г. в местности хантингтон, хирефордшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 39 лет и был похоронен в местности Англия, Великобритания.

Se casó con Anna Underhill el 3 de agosto de 1620, en Hail Weston, Huntingdonshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas.
